title: Construct the witnessing structure A with a computable copy

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Sub-lemma (G1).** Produce an explicit, computably-represented countable structure $A$ that will witness Question 1.2.

Requirements:
- Universe is $\N$; all interpreted constants/functions/relations are computable (problem demands *computably-represented*).
- $A$ must be designed with infinitely many *independent* automorphism degrees of freedom (e.g. a disjoint family of gadgets $G_0,G_1,\dots$ each carrying a small nontrivial automorphism), while still keeping $\AUT(A)$ countable (see companion issue G2).

A natural template: $A=\bigsqcup_n G_n$ with each $\AUT(G_n)\cong \Z/2$ generated by an involution $\tau_n$ swapping a 'twin' pair $\{p_n,q_n\}$ that cannot be existentially distinguished without naming an external base point. The construction must be pinned down precisely (signature, interpretations) — no placeholders.

This is the foundational object on which both the positive clause (P1) and the diagonal clause (P2) depend.
